findings: Destination Port feature exhibits moderate shortcut-like predictive leverage as indicated by deterministic one-feature decision stump analysis.
Class-conditioned distribution differences with dominant ratio 1.0 and zero entropy for DDoS class support the presence of shortcut signals.
Moderate entropy and dominant ratio for BENIGN class and high JS divergence further support representation-sensitive shortcut-like patterns.
limitations: Shortcut verification limited to one-feature decision stump; more complex interactions not assessed.
Moderate signal strength leaves some uncertainty about the full extent of shortcut-like leverage.
